description_long: This kiosk is a small, weathered structure with a gabled roof and a partially open front, suggesting it serves as a point-of-sale or information booth. The exterior is primarily constructed from corrugated metal or wood, with visible signs of rust and aging, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. There are several signs and posters attached to the front and side, including a blue sign and a white placard, indicating commercial or informational use. The front features a large open window and a counter area, with a small mailbox or drop box and a post in front. The roof has a slight overhang, providing some shelter to the front area. The ground around the kiosk appears to be paved or covered in a light material, possibly concrete or gravel.
description: A small, rustic kiosk with a gabled roof, open front window, and multiple signs, showing signs of age and weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open window, counter, signage, and posts in front of the kiosk.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the overhanging roof, and the side signage.
description_view_2: Rear view with the gabled roof and back wall, less signage visible, and a clear view of the structure's weathered materials.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side wall, roof overhang, and partial view of the front opening.
attribute_signage_type: Printed signs and placards, attached to the front and side panels, including a blue sign and white placard with text.
attribute_window_presence: One large rectangular window at the front.
attribute_door_configuration: Single open entryway at the front, no visible door.
attribute_roof_design: Gabled roof with moderate overhang, constructed from corrugated metal or wood, visibly weathered.
attribute_architectural_style: Rustic utilitarian with simple, functional design and visible aging.
